industryProblem.title: The security industry has a dirty secret.
industryProblem.p1: $200 billion is spent on cybersecurity every year. But who does it protect? Fortune 500 companies. Banks. Governments.
industryProblem.p2: Meanwhile, you — a three-person startup, a freelance developer, an e-commerce store with its first 100 customers — you get nothing.
industryProblem.p3: Not because you don't care about security. Because security was never built for you.
industryProblem.p4: Enterprise solutions start at six figures a year. And they still need a dedicated team to operate.
industryProblem.emphasis: This industry turned security into a luxury. In a world where AI agents are deployed every second, that's unacceptable.

notAntivirus.title: "Can't I just install antivirus?"
notAntivirus.subtitle: Antivirus scans files for known malware signatures. Panguard is a completely different class of tool — it monitors behavior, deploys decoys, and uses AI to catch what signatures miss.
notAntivirus.footnote: Think of it this way: antivirus is a door lock. Panguard is the full building security system — cameras, motion sensors, decoy rooms, and a 24/7 AI guard.

funnel.title: 90% of threats don't need AI.

confidence.title: Smart enough to act. Honest enough to ask.

Asks you first. "Should I block this? Here's what I found."
Just tells you. "Something looks off. Here's the evidence."

confidence.footer: At 3 AM, you don't want AI making a decision it's only 65% sure about.

threatCloud.title: The more people use it, the safer everyone gets.
threatCloud.desc: Every Panguard instance is a sensor. When it detects a new attack, it anonymously shares the pattern with Threat Cloud. Threat Cloud doesn't just generate traditional Sigma detection rules — it also produces ATR (Agent Threat Rules), the world's first open standard for detecting AI agent attacks. Prompt injection, tool poisoning, context exfiltration — threats Sigma can't catch, ATR catches.
threatCloud.descHighlight: You're not just protected. You're part of the shield.
threatCloud.privacy1: Only anonymized threat signatures leave your machine. Zero raw data. Zero telemetry. Encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3). Your logs, your code, your data — never leaves.
threatCloud.privacy2: You can turn off Threat Cloud anytime. Even Community tier works fully offline.

faq.faq1a: Every new account gets a 14-day free trial of the Solo plan with full access to all Solo features. No credit card required. After the trial ends, your account automatically downgrades to the free Community plan. You can upgrade to a paid plan at any time.
faq.faq2q: Can I switch plans at any time?
faq.faq2a: Absolutely. You can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any point. When upgrading, you get immediate access to the new features and we prorate the difference. When downgrading, the change takes effect at the start of your next billing cycle.
faq.faq3a: A machine is any device or server running the Panguard agent. This includes workstations, laptops, cloud VMs, and bare-metal servers. Containers within a single host count as one machine. Deactivated agents do not count toward your limit.
faq.faq4q: Do you offer annual billing discounts?
faq.faq4a: Yes. Annual billing saves you 20% compared to monthly pricing. For example, Solo drops from $9/mo to $7.20/mo, Pro from $29/mo to $23.20/mo, and Business from $79/mo to $63.20/mo -- all billed annually.
faq.faq5q: Is the Community plan really free?
faq.faq5a: Yes, forever. Panguard is open source (MIT). The Community plan includes full security scanning, real-time Layer 1 protection, and community support for 1 machine. When you need multi-machine support, AI defense, or compliance reports, upgrade starting at $9/mo.
faq.faq6a: Available on Pro and Business plans. Generate Taiwan Cyber Security Act ($299), ISO 27001 ($499), SOC 2 ($699), or bundle all three ($999). Reports are AI-powered gap analysis delivered in PDF + JSON format.
faq.faq7q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
faq.faq7a: Your data remains accessible in read-only mode for 30 days after cancellation. During that window, you can export all reports, logs, and configurations. After 30 days, data is securely deleted in accordance with our data retention policy and GDPR requirements.
faq.faq8q: How does the compliance report quality compare to a human consultant?
faq.faq8a: Panguard compliance reports evaluate 30 ISO 27001 controls, 10 SOC 2 controls, and 10 TCSA controls, automatically scanning your system configuration and assessing each control. Report format meets audit standards. However, we recommend using the report as a starting point, working with your internal team or external consultant to complete the final certification process.

features.title: What Panguard Scan does.
features.subtitle: A comprehensive security snapshot without any permanent installation.

item1.title: 7 Built-in Scanners
item1.desc: Password policy audit, unnecessary port detection, SSL certificate validation, scheduled task review, shared folder exposure check, environment discovery, and CVE vulnerability lookup. Each scanner runs independently and produces actionable findings.

item2.title: PDF Report with Compliance Mapping
item2.desc: Every scan generates a branded PDF: cover page, executive summary, findings table sorted by severity, compliance control mapping (ISO 27001, SOC 2), and step-by-step remediation guidance.

item3.title: Quick & Full Scan Modes
item3.desc: Quick scan hits the most critical checks in under 60 seconds. Full scan runs all 7 scanner modules for a comprehensive audit. Both modes produce a complete PDF report.

item4.title: Open Port & Service Analysis
item4.desc: Discovers all listening ports, identifies the services behind them, and flags unnecessary exposure. Detects common misconfigurations like exposed databases, admin panels, and dev tools left in production.

item5.title: SSL & Configuration Audit
item5.desc: Validates SSL certificates (expiry, chain, protocol versions), checks password policies, reviews scheduled tasks for persistence mechanisms, and audits shared folder permissions.

item6.title: Environment Discovery
item6.desc: Automatically identifies your OS, distribution, kernel version, installed packages, and running services. Builds a complete inventory that feeds into the compliance mapping engine.

item1.title: Solo Developer
item1.desc: You just deployed a new VPS for your side project. Before you share the URL, run a Panguard Scan to make sure you did not accidentally expose your database port or leave SSH password auth enabled.

problem.title: A scan tells you what is wrong. It does not fix it.
problem.desc: Scanning once is better than nothing. But threats do not wait for your next scan. Attackers probe your systems 24/7. New vulnerabilities are published daily. Configuration drift happens with every deployment. You need continuous protection, not periodic checkups.
problem.desc2: Panguard Guard is the permanent security agent that lives on your server, watches everything in real-time, and takes action before you even know there is a problem. It installs in one command, runs in the background, and tells you about threats in plain language.

features.title: What Panguard Guard does.
features.subtitle: Enterprise-grade endpoint protection, designed for teams that do not have a dedicated security team.

item1.title: 5-Agent AI Pipeline
item1.desc: Events flow through 5 specialized AI agents: Detect (Sigma/YARA rules + local LLM), Analyze (threat classification), Respond (auto-remediation), Report (compliance documentation), and Chat (Telegram, Slack, email notifications). 90% of events handled at zero cost by rules alone.

item2.title: 3 Auto-Response Types
item2.desc: High-confidence threats are neutralized automatically via three response modules: IP Blocker (firewall rules), Process Killer (malicious process termination), and File Quarantine (suspicious file isolation). Every action is logged and reversible.

item3.title: 4 Notification Channels
item3.desc: Get alerts where you already are: Telegram, Slack, Email, and Webhook. Each channel supports rich formatting with threat details, severity badges, and one-click remediation links.

item4.title: Investigation Engine
item4.desc: Built-in investigation engine with dynamic reasoning, event correlation across a sliding window (500 events, 10-minute window), and multi-tool analysis. Correlates events by IP, user, process, and category. Risk scoring with confidence-based escalation.

item5.title: Graceful Degradation
item5.desc: Cloud AI down? Local LLM (Ollama) takes over. LLM offline? The rule engine with 3,155 Sigma and 5,895 YARA rules handles it. Guard never stops protecting, regardless of network conditions.

item6.title: Integration Adapters
item6.desc: Pre-built adapters for Windows Defender, Syslog, and Wazuh. 7-day learning period builds a behavioral baseline for your system. Supports Linux, macOS, Windows, Docker, and Kubernetes.

item7.title: SOAR Playbooks
item7.desc: Define automated response strategies in YAML. Three built-in playbooks for brute-force, port-scan, and data-exfiltration. Match correlation patterns, escalate to tickets, and run multi-step response sequences.

item8.title: Log Collectors
item8.desc: Ingest security events from syslog (RFC 3164/5424), auth logs, and custom log files. Four built-in parsers with automatic format detection. Feed events directly into the detection pipeline.

item9.title: Anomaly Scoring
item9.desc: Welford's online algorithm builds statistical baselines for every metric. Z-score deviation detection identifies anomalies without manual threshold tuning. Adapts continuously to your environment.

item10.title: Investigation Engine
item10.desc: Dynamic reasoning with 8 investigation tools: IP history, user privilege, time anomaly, geolocation, related events, process tree, file reputation, and network pattern. AI-generated investigation plans with evidence collection.

learningPeriod.title: Smart Learning Period
learningPeriod.desc: After installation, Guard spends 7 days learning your system's normal behavior -- which processes run, what traffic is normal, when files change. During the learning period, all detections are logged but nothing is blocked. After 7 days, Guard automatically switches to protection mode and begins actively responding to threats.

problem.title: Security alerts are useless if nobody understands them.
problem.desc: Traditional security tools drown you in alerts. CVE numbers, severity matrices, MITRE ATT&CK framework references -- all designed for security professionals who do not exist in most small teams. The result? Alerts get ignored. Important warnings are lost in noise. Breaches happen not because they were undetected, but because nobody understood the detection.
problem.desc2: Panguard Chat translates every security event into language any human can understand. It tells you what happened, why it matters, and what to do about it. And it does it in the messaging platform you already have open.

features.title: What Panguard Chat does.
features.subtitle: The communication layer for your entire security operation.

item1.title: Natural Language Notifications
item1.desc: No more cryptic alert codes. When Panguard detects something, it tells you in plain language: 'Someone attempted to brute-force your SSH from an IP in Brazil at 2:47 AM. We blocked it. No action needed.'

item2.title: Smart Alert Summaries
item2.desc: Every alert includes full context: what happened, where, when, severity level, and what Panguard did about it. Weekly and daily digests summarize your security posture so you never miss the big picture.

item3.title: 4 Platform Channels
item3.desc: Telegram, Slack, Email, and Webhook. Panguard Chat works where you already are. No separate dashboard to check. Security updates arrive in the channel you already monitor.

item4.title: Role-Adaptive Tone
item4.desc: Chat adapts its language based on who is reading. Developers get technical details with CLI commands. Business owners get plain summaries. IT admins get actionable steps. Set your role once, get the right level of detail every time.

item5.title: 13 Built-in Skills
item5.desc: Chat Agent has 13 skills across 6 categories: scan (quick scan, compliance scan), guard (status, block/unblock IP), trap (status, top attackers), report (generate, summary), system (health, config), and info (explain events, help). NLP matching routes your messages to the right skill automatically.

item6.title: Team Collaboration
item6.desc: Tag teammates, escalate incidents to specific people, and maintain a conversation history that serves as an audit trail. Everyone on the team gets the context they need without switching tools.

inAction.title: Proactive alerts, not another dashboard.
inAction.desc: Panguard Chat is not a dashboard you have to remember to check. It reaches out to you proactively with weekly summaries, real-time alerts, and incident walkthroughs -- all in the messaging platform you already use.

trap.title: Intelligent honeypots.
trap.subtitle: Turn the tables. Let attackers reveal themselves while your real systems stay protected.

problem.title: Defensive security is reactive. You are always one step behind.
problem.desc: Traditional security waits for attackers to hit your real systems before responding. By then, you are already at a disadvantage. You do not know what tools they are using, what they are looking for, or how sophisticated they are. You are defending blind.
problem.desc2: Panguard Trap flips the script. It deploys realistic decoy services that attract attackers and capture their every move. While they waste time on fake targets, you gather intelligence that makes your real defenses stronger. The attacker becomes the informant.

step1.label: Deploy
step1.desc: Panguard Trap identifies unused ports and deploys realistic decoy services automatically.

step2.label: Attract
step2.desc: Attackers scanning your network discover the honeypots and interact with them, thinking they are real.

step3.label: Capture
step3.desc: Every command, file upload, and lateral movement attempt is recorded in a sandboxed environment.

step4.label: Analyze
step4.desc: AI classifies the attack, profiles the attacker, and extracts indicators of compromise (IOCs).

step5.label: Share
step5.desc: Anonymized threat intelligence is pushed to the collective network, strengthening every Panguard user.

features.title: What Panguard Trap does.
features.subtitle: Proactive threat intelligence that makes your defenses smarter every day.

item1.title: 8 Honeypot Types
item1.desc: SSH, HTTP, FTP, SMB, MySQL, RDP, Telnet, and Redis. Panguard Trap deploys realistic decoy services on unused ports. Each honeypot type mimics real service behavior to capture attacker techniques.

item2.title: Attacker Profiling
item2.desc: Every interaction is logged in detail: tools used, commands executed, files uploaded, lateral movement attempts. AI classifies attacker skill level (script kiddie, intermediate, advanced), intent, and tool signatures.

item3.title: Collective Threat Intelligence
item3.desc: Attack patterns captured by your traps are anonymized and contributed to the Panguard collective intelligence network via the Threat Cloud API. Your data protects every other user -- and theirs protects you.

item4.title: AI-Powered Analysis
item4.desc: Captured attacker sessions are analyzed by Panguard's AI to classify attack types, estimate sophistication levels, and identify whether the attacker is an automated scanner or a human operator.

item5.title: Flexible Deployment
item5.desc: Choose which honeypot services to deploy based on your threat landscape. Running a web server? Deploy HTTP and MySQL traps. Seeing SSH probes? Add an SSH honeypot. Configure the service mix to match your environment and gather the most relevant intelligence.

item6.title: Zero Risk to Production
item6.desc: Honeypots are completely isolated from your production environment. They run in sandboxed containers with no access to real data, real services, or real network segments. Attackers waste their time on decoys.

item1.title: Threat Researcher
item1.desc: You want to understand what attackers are doing to systems like yours. Deploy Panguard Trap and receive detailed reports on attacker behavior, tools, and techniques. Use the intelligence to harden your real systems.

item2.title: Security Team
item2.desc: Your team uses honeypot data to train detection models and validate security controls. Panguard Trap provides a continuous stream of real attacker behavior that is far more valuable than synthetic test data.

item3.title: Compliance Requirement
item3.desc: Some compliance frameworks require active threat detection beyond passive monitoring. Honeypots demonstrate proactive security posture to auditors and provide evidence of continuous threat awareness.

cta.title: Stop guessing. Start knowing.
cta.desc: Deploy intelligent honeypots and turn attacker activity into actionable intelligence.

skillAuditor.title: The security layer that runs
skillAuditor.subtitle: Every time you install a skill from OpenClaw, Panguard scans it automatically. Clean? It installs. Suspicious? You get a report on your phone before anything happens.

problem.title: AI agent skills are the new attack surface.
problem.desc: OpenClaw, ClawdHub, and MCP marketplaces make it easy to install powerful skills into your AI agent. But every skill you install is code that runs with your agent's permissions — accessing your files, your environment variables, your servers.
problem.desc2: A single malicious skill can exfiltrate credentials, open reverse shells, or hijack your agent's identity. Manual review can't catch zero-width Unicode, encoded payloads, or sophisticated prompt injection.

preInstall.title: Pre-Install Gate

cicd.title: CI/CD Pipeline Ready
cicd.subtitle: Automate your security gate

toolPoisoning.name: Tool Poisoning

name.title: PANGUARD
name.etymology: Pan (Greek god of shepherds, meaning universal) + Guard
name.desc1: In Greek mythology, Pan was the god of the wild, shepherds, and flocks. He watched over everything that lived beyond the city walls -- the vulnerable, the unprotected, the overlooked.
name.desc2a: That is exactly what Panguard does. The Fortune 500 have walls, moats, and armies of security engineers. The solo developer, the five-person startup, the small business owner -- they have nothing.
name.desc2b: We are their shepherd. We are the guard that watches the wilderness.

values.title: What we believe.
values.subtitle: These are not slogans on a wall. They are decision-making frameworks that shape every product choice, every design review, and every line of code.

item1.title: Users don't think
item1.subtitle: Zero friction
item1.desc: Security should not require a PhD. If the user has to configure it, we failed. If the user has to read a manual, we failed. One command to install, zero configuration, and it just works.

item2.title: Human language first
item2.subtitle: No jargon
item2.desc: We never show alert codes when we can show plain English. 'Someone tried to access your SSH port from Russia at 3 AM' is infinitely more useful than 'CVE-2024-XXXX detected on port 22'. Security that speaks human.

item3.title: Security never stops
item3.subtitle: Graceful degradation
item3.desc: Cloud down? Local AI takes over. LLM offline? Rule engine handles it. Panguard never says 'service unavailable.' Protection degrades in capability, never in availability. The watchdog always watches.

item4.title: Collective defense

item4.desc: When one Panguard agent learns something new, every agent benefits. A solo developer in Taipei and a small business in Berlin share the same threat intelligence, anonymously. The network grows stronger with every node.

problem.desc: Getting SOC 2 certified costs $20,000 to $60,000 in consultant fees. ISO 27001 is even more. In Taiwan, TCSA compliance engagements run NT$300,000 to NT$800,000. The process takes months, requires dedicated staff, and the documentation is outdated the moment the audit ends.
problem.desc2: Panguard already monitors your systems 24/7 -- it has all the data an auditor needs. Our compliance engine takes that telemetry and automatically maps it to framework controls, generating audit-ready documentation that stays current every single day.

iso27001.panguardDoes: Maps findings to 114 Annex A controls across organizational and technical themes against your live security telemetry. Generates gap analysis with specific remediation steps.

soc2.name: SOC 2 Type II

soc2.whatItCovers: Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy trust service criteria with continuous evidence collection.
soc2.panguardDoes: Maps findings to 64 trust service controls with automated evidence collection. Generates audit-ready documentation packages including timestamped logs, configuration snapshots, and AI reasoning chains.

tcsa.name: Taiwan Cyber Security Act
tcsa.whoNeeds: Government contractors, critical infrastructure providers, and any Taiwan-based organization subject to the Cyber Security Management Act.
tcsa.whatItCovers: Security monitoring, incident reporting, infrastructure protection, risk assessment, and compliance reporting for government-regulated entities.
tcsa.panguardDoes: Full bilingual output (English + Traditional Chinese). Maps findings to 38 controls covering monitoring, incident reporting, and compliance. Reports formatted for regulatory submission.

security.title: We're a security company.
security.subtitle: You trust us to protect your infrastructure. That means we hold ourselves to a higher standard than we hold anyone else. Here is exactly how we do it.

practices.title: How we secure ourselves.
practices.subtitle: These are not aspirational goals. They are current, enforced practices that apply to every line of code, every deployment, and every employee.

item1.title: Single-Tenant Architecture
item1.desc: Every customer deployment runs in an isolated environment. There is no shared database, no shared compute, and no shared network segment. A breach in one tenant cannot propagate to another.

item2.title: End-to-End Encryption
item2.desc: All data is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). Encryption keys are managed per-tenant with automatic rotation every 90 days. Key material never leaves the hardware security module.

item3.title: Zero Data Retention
item3.desc: Cloud AI queries are ephemeral. Payloads sent to Claude or GPT are not stored, not used for training, and not logged beyond the request lifecycle. PII is stripped before any data leaves the device.

item4.title: Auditable AI Decisions
item4.desc: Every automated action taken by Panguard is logged with a full reasoning chain. You can trace exactly why an event was flagged, what confidence score it received, and what response was executed.

item5.title: Continuous Penetration Testing
item5.desc: We engage independent third-party security firms to conduct penetration tests on a quarterly basis. Critical findings are remediated within 48 hours. Test results are available to enterprise customers upon request.

item6.title: Secure Development Lifecycle
item6.desc: Every code change goes through automated SAST/DAST scanning, dependency auditing, and peer review. We follow OWASP best practices and maintain a bug bounty program for external researchers.

compliance.title: Frameworks we follow.
compliance.subtitle: Compliance is not a checkbox exercise. It is the minimum bar. We build to the spirit of these frameworks, not just their letter.

frameworks.description: We are actively pursuing SOC 2 Type II certification covering Security, Availability, and Confidentiality trust service criteria. Our audit is conducted by a Big Four firm. Expected completion: Q3 2026.

ISO 27001 certification is on our roadmap for 2026. Our information security management system (ISMS) is being built to ISO 27001 standards from day one, making certification a formalization rather than a transformation.

Panguard is designed for GDPR compliance by default. Data minimization, purpose limitation, and the right to erasure are built into the architecture. We offer Data Processing Agreements (DPA) to all customers.
For customers operating under Taiwan's Cybersecurity Management Act, Panguard's reporting and audit capabilities are designed to meet regulatory requirements for critical infrastructure providers.

dataHandling.subtitle: Transparency about data flows is fundamental. Here is a complete breakdown of where your data lives and what -- if anything -- leaves the device.

items: Full event history and forensic logs

anonymization.title: Anonymization Pipeline
anonymization.desc1: Before any event data is sent to cloud AI or the collective intelligence network, it passes through a multi-stage anonymization pipeline. IP addresses are hashed, hostnames are replaced with generic identifiers, file paths are normalized, and user data is removed entirely.
anonymization.desc2: The pipeline is deterministic, so the same threat pattern always produces the same anonymized signature -- enabling correlation without exposing identity.

disclosure.title: Responsible Disclosure
disclosure.desc1: Found a vulnerability in Panguard? We appreciate security researchers who help us keep our users safe. Please report any security issues through our responsible disclosure program.
disclosure.desc2: We commit to acknowledging reports within 24 hours and providing an initial assessment within 72 hours.

encryption.subtitle: How we protect data at rest, in transit, and track every access.

0.title: Data in Transit
0.desc: TLS 1.3 for all API communication. Certificate pinning for Threat Cloud connections. HTTPS enforced with HSTS.

1.title: Data at Rest
1.desc: AES-256 encryption for credential storage. Configuration files with restricted permissions (0600). Baseline data stored locally, never transmitted.

2.title: Audit Logging
2.desc: Every detection, response action, and configuration change is logged with timestamp, source, and actor. Logs stored locally with optional syslog forwarding.

3.title: Permission Model

technology.title: Security that
technology.subtitle: A three-layer AI defense funnel, five autonomous agents, and a context memory that learns your environment. Built so the 90 % of events that are noise cost nothing, and the 3 % that matter get the deepest reasoning available.

defenseFunnel.title: Three layers. 90 % free.
defenseFunnel.subtitle: Events flow downward through increasingly powerful -- and increasingly expensive -- analysis layers. The funnel ensures cost efficiency while guaranteeing that no genuine threat is missed.

layer1.name: Rule Engine
layer1.desc: Open-source Sigma and YARA rules form the bedrock. They process the vast majority of security events instantly, on-device, with zero cost per event. New community rules are pulled daily from curated feeds and automatically compiled into the local engine.

layer2.name: Edge AI
layer2.desc: Events that rules cannot confidently classify are escalated to a local large-language model running on Ollama. This keeps sensitive data on the device, avoids network latency, and adds contextual reasoning without cloud dependency.

layer3.name: Cloud AI
layer3.desc: Only the most ambiguous or novel threats reach cloud AI for deep reasoning. The payload is scrubbed of PII before transmission. Cloud AI returns a structured verdict with a confidence score and a plain-language explanation.

agents.title: Five agents. One mission.
agents.subtitle: Each agent is a specialist. Together they form an autonomous security operations pipeline that detects, analyzes, responds, reports, and notifies -- keeping you informed in real time.

detect.name: Detect Agent
detect.desc: Continuously monitors system logs, network traffic, and file-system changes. Applies Sigma and YARA rules in real-time, flagging anomalies the moment they appear. It produces raw event signals enriched with MITRE ATT&CK TTP tags.

analyze.name: Analyze Agent
analyze.desc: Receives flagged events from the Detect Agent and performs multi-step reasoning. It correlates events across time, queries the Context Memory for baseline deviations, and assigns a confidence score from 0 to 100.

respond.name: Respond Agent
respond.desc: Executes response playbooks based on confidence thresholds. High-confidence threats trigger automatic isolation, firewall rule injection, or process termination. Medium-confidence events queue human-review tasks with full context.

report.name: Report Agent
report.desc: Transforms raw incident data into structured reports mapped to ISO 27001, SOC 2, and other frameworks. Generates executive summaries, timeline visualizations, and audit-ready evidence packages automatically.

chat.name: Chat Agent
chat.desc: The human interface. Users ask questions in plain language and receive answers backed by real telemetry. Integrated with Slack and Telegram. Sends proactive weekly summaries and real-time breach notifications.

contextMemory.title: Seven days to learn you. Then it never forgets.
contextMemory.desc1: During the first seven days after installation, Panguard silently observes your system: normal network patterns, typical process trees, expected cron schedules, and standard user behaviour. This builds a per-device baseline stored in an encrypted local database.
contextMemory.desc2: After the learning window, any deviation from baseline is scored and flagged. The model continually refines itself -- a new legitimate service gets adopted into the baseline within hours, while a novel attack pattern triggers escalation immediately.

phase1.label: Observation
phase1.desc: Collecting process trees, network connections, file-system baselines

phase2.label: Pattern extraction
phase2.desc: Building statistical models of normal behavior per service

phase3.label: Threshold tuning
phase3.desc: Calibrating alert thresholds to minimize false positives

phase4.label: Active protection
phase4.desc: Full detection + auto-response with continuous refinement

confidenceScoring.title: Every event gets a score.
confidenceScoring.subtitle: A 0-100 confidence score determines what happens next. High scores trigger automatic response. Medium scores notify humans. Low scores feed the learning system.

high.label: Auto-respond
high.desc: High-confidence threats are neutralized automatically. The Respond Agent executes the matching playbook within seconds, then logs every action for audit.

medium.label: Notify & Review
medium.desc: Medium-confidence events trigger a notification to the designated human reviewer via Chat Agent. Full context and AI reasoning are attached so the reviewer can approve or dismiss in one click.

low.label: Log & Learn
low.desc: Low-confidence signals are logged with full metadata and fed into the Context Memory system. Over time, the baseline model refines itself and these signals either graduate to higher bands or are suppressed as noise.

collectiveIntelligence.title: One device detects it. Every device blocks it.
collectiveIntelligence.desc1: When a Panguard agent identifies a previously unknown threat, an anonymous indicator of compromise (IOC) is contributed to the collective intelligence network. Within minutes, every other Panguard agent receives the new signature.
collectiveIntelligence.desc2: This creates a feedback loop: the more devices in the network, the faster new threats are caught, and the stronger every individual agent becomes. A small business with one server benefits from threat data generated across the entire Panguard fleet.

item1.label: Anonymous sharing
item1.desc: Threat indicators are stripped of all identifying data before contribution.

item2.label: Distributed cache
item2.desc: New threat signatures propagate to the entire fleet within minutes.

item3.label: Automatic rule push
item3.desc: Community-validated signatures are compiled into Sigma/YARA rules and pushed to every agent.

item4.label: Privacy-first
item4.desc: No IP addresses, hostnames, or user data leave the device. Only hashes and behavioral patterns.

resilience.title: Security never stops.
resilience.subtitle: Network down? API tokens depleted? Cloud provider outage? Panguard degrades gracefully through its three layers. Protection is always on.

optimal.desc: Cloud AI + Local LLM + Rule Engine -- full three-layer analysis on every event.

cloudUnavailable.desc: Local LLM + Rule Engine. Complex events queue for cloud retry. No gaps in protection.

llmOffline.desc: Rule Engine only. Sigma + YARA still catch 90 % of known threats. Events are logged for later AI analysis.

emergency.desc: Core watchdog process monitors critical signals. If Panguard itself is targeted, the watchdog alerts the owner and preserves forensic logs.

stack.title: Built on proven foundations.
stack.subtitle: Every component is chosen for reliability, performance, and developer ergonomics. No proprietary lock-in.

item1.name: TypeScript
item1.desc: End-to-end type safety

item2.name: Sigma Rules
item2.desc: Industry-standard detection

item3.name: YARA Rules
item3.desc: Malware pattern matching

item11.name: MCP Protocol
item11.desc: Model Context Protocol for AI assistant integration

item12.name: Semgrep
item12.desc: Static analysis for SAST code scanning

defenseStack.title: Five layers of real-time defense.
defenseStack.subtitle: Each layer instruments a different attack surface with battle-tested open-source engines. From kernel syscalls to log correlation, every layer feeds normalized events into the AI pipeline.

kernel.name: Kernel Monitoring
kernel.desc: Hooks directly into Linux kernel syscalls via eBPF probes. Detects container escapes, reverse shells, cryptomining processes, credential access, and anomalous file descriptors -- all without kernel modules or system overhead.
kernel.detects: Container escapes, reverse shells, privilege escalation, suspicious exec() chains
kernel.tech: eBPF tracepoints on sys_enter/sys_exit, Falco rules compiled to BPF bytecode, <1ms per-event latency

network.name: Network IDS
network.desc: Deep packet inspection on all network interfaces. Suricata's EVE JSON output feeds protocol-decoded alerts -- including TLS fingerprinting, DNS anomaly detection, and HTTP payload analysis -- directly into the event pipeline.
network.detects: C2 beaconing, data exfiltration, lateral movement, exploit payloads, protocol anomalies
network.tech: Multi-threaded pcap analysis, 40+ protocol decoders, ET Open + custom rulesets, flow tracking

filesystem.name: File System Scanning
filesystem.desc: On-access and scheduled scanning of file system changes. YARA rules match byte patterns, PE headers, and string signatures against known malware families, web shells, and suspicious scripts.
filesystem.detects: Malware binaries, web shells, ransomware droppers, obfuscated scripts, credential harvesters
filesystem.tech: Pattern-based signature matching, custom + community YARA rulesets, inotify-triggered scanning

logs.name: Log Correlation
logs.desc: Sigma detection rules correlate events across system logs, auth logs, application logs, and cloud audit trails. The rule engine supports temporal correlation, aggregation, and multi-source joins.
logs.detects: Brute force attacks, lateral movement chains, persistence mechanisms, policy violations
logs.tech: 2,500+ community Sigma rules, hot-reload, MITRE ATT&CK TTP mapping, custom rule authoring

ai.name: AI Correlation
ai.desc: Events that pass through layers 1-4 are cross-correlated by AI. The model weighs behavioral baselines, threat intelligence feeds, and temporal patterns to produce a confidence-scored verdict with full attack narrative.
ai.detects: Zero-day attacks, APT campaigns, novel attack chains, false positive reduction
ai.tech: Local Ollama + Cloud LLM fallback, RAG over baseline memory, threat intel enrichment, 0-100 scoring

dataFlow.title: From raw signal to actionable alert.
dataFlow.subtitle: Every security event traverses a five-stage pipeline. Data is normalized, enriched, correlated, scored, and dispatched -- typically in under 200ms.

stage1.name: Ingest
stage1.desc: Raw events from Falco, Suricata, YARA, file watchers, and process monitors are captured in real-time.

stage2.name: Normalize
stage2.desc: Events are mapped to a unified schema with source, severity, category, and MITRE ATT&CK tags.

stage3.name: Correlate
stage3.desc: Sigma rules and AI cross-reference events against baseline behavior and threat intelligence feeds.

stage4.name: Score
stage4.desc: Each correlated event receives a 0-100 confidence score determining automated response thresholds.

stage5.name: Alert
stage5.desc: Verdicts trigger playbook execution and dispatch to Slack, Telegram, Email, and the dashboard.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started with Panguard
gettingStarted.subtitle: Install Panguard, run your first scan, and enable real-time protection in under 5 minutes.
gettingStarted.req1: Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 11+, CentOS 8+) or macOS 12+. Windows requires WSL2
gettingStarted.req3: 4 GB RAM recommended + 500 MB disk space
gettingStarted.step1Desc: Install via npm or use the install script. Both methods set up the CLI, rule engine, and local AI model.
gettingStarted.step1Note: The installer auto-detects your OS and architecture. Expected output:
gettingStarted.step2Desc: A quick scan checks your system for common vulnerabilities in about 60 seconds.
gettingStarted.step2Deep: For a comprehensive analysis including all ports, SSL certificates, and configuration files:
gettingStarted.step3Desc: Guard runs as a daemon, monitoring your system 24/7 with Sigma and YARA rules.
gettingStarted.step4Desc: Panguard Chat sends you plain-language notifications when threats are detected and resolved.
gettingStarted.step5Desc: Every scan produces a security score, grade, and detailed findings. Here's how to read them.
gettingStarted.step5Score: Security Score (0-100): 0 = critical risk, 100 = fully protected. Anything below 60 needs immediate attention.
gettingStarted.step5Grade: Grade: A (90+), B (75-89), C (60-74), D (40-59), F (below 40). Target grade B or higher.
gettingStarted.step5Findings: Findings are sorted by severity: Critical > High > Medium > Low. Fix critical issues first.
gettingStarted.step6Title: Step 6: JSON Output for AI Agents
gettingStarted.step6Desc: Panguard is Agent-Native. Use --json to get machine-readable output that AI agents can parse directly.
gettingStarted.step6Note: When --json is active, no spinners, banners, or colors are output. Pure JSON only.
gettingStarted.step6Integration: Your AI agent can call Panguard via subprocess and parse the JSON response to make security decisions autonomously.
gettingStarted.step7Desc: Scan any server or domain from outside. Panguard checks open ports, SSL certificates, HTTP headers, and DNS records.
gettingStarted.step7Note: Remote scans use Node.js built-in modules only. No nmap required. Combine with --json for automated monitoring.
gettingStarted.step8Desc: Generate ISO 27001, SOC 2, or Taiwan Cyber Security Act compliance reports automatically. Requires Pro plan.
gettingStarted.step8Frameworks: Supported frameworks: ISO 27001 Annex A, SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria, Taiwan TCSA (ISMS).
gettingStarted.step8Note: Reports can be generated in English or Traditional Chinese with PDF export.
gettingStarted.step9Desc: Panguard has a full CLI toolkit. Here are the most useful commands beyond scan and guard.
gettingStarted.step9Whoami: Check your account and subscription status:
gettingStarted.step9Chat: Set up notification channels (Telegram, Slack, email):

gettingStarted.checklistDesc: Follow these steps to fully deploy Panguard. Each step is independent -- enable only what you need.

cl1.title: 1. Security Scan -- Get your security score
cl1.desc: Scan your system for vulnerabilities and get a security score. Quick mode ~30s, full mode ~60s.

cl2.title: 2. Real-time Protection -- Start the Guard daemon
cl2.desc: Guard monitors your system 24/7. First 7 days in learning mode (detect + log), then auto-switches to protection mode (detect + block).

cl3.title: 3. Alert Notifications -- Set up Telegram / Slack
cl3.desc: Get notified instantly when threats are detected. Supports Telegram, Slack, Email, and Webhook.

cl4.title: 4. Honeypots (Optional) -- Deploy decoy services
cl4.desc: Deploy fake SSH, HTTP, MySQL services on your server to capture attacker behavior and intent.

cl5.title: 5. Compliance Reports (Optional) -- Generate reports
cl5.desc: Auto-generate ISO 27001, SOC 2, or Taiwan Cyber Security Act compliance reports. Use as audit evidence.

gettingStarted.advancedHintDesc: Panguard works out of the box, but you can enhance detection with optional external tools. All tools degrade gracefully -- Panguard never stops working if an external tool is unavailable.
gettingStarted.advancedHintOllama: Ollama -- Run AI analysis locally without cloud APIs. Ideal for air-gapped environments.
gettingStarted.advancedHintFalco: Falco -- Linux kernel-level syscall monitoring via eBPF for deep behavioral detection.
gettingStarted.advancedHintSuricata: Suricata -- Network-layer IDS/IPS for advanced traffic analysis.
gettingStarted.advancedHintEnv: Key environment variables: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (Claude AI), OPENAI_API_KEY (GPT fallback), PANGUARD_LLM_MODEL (Ollama model override), OLLAMA_API_ENDPOINT (custom Ollama URL).
gettingStarted.advancedHintLink: See the full Advanced Setup guide for installation instructions and configuration details.
gettingStarted.multiEndpointDesc: Deploying Panguard across multiple servers is straightforward. Use SSH loops for small fleets or Ansible for larger environments.
gettingStarted.multiEndpointAnsibleHint: For 20+ servers, consider using an Ansible playbook for idempotent, repeatable deployment.
gettingStarted.multiEndpointLink: See the full Multi-Endpoint Deployment guide for Ansible playbook examples and Threat Cloud integration.

advancedSetup.title: Advanced Setup
advancedSetup.subtitle: Enhance Panguard's detection capabilities with optional external tools. All tools are optional -- Panguard works fully without them.
advancedSetup.degradeDesc: Panguard's AI analysis follows a three-tier degradation strategy: Cloud AI (Claude/OpenAI) > Local LLM (Ollama) > Rule Engine (Sigma + YARA). Each tier is independent. Even with all external tools offline, 3,155 Sigma rules and 5,895 YARA rules still provide full protection.

skillAuditorDocs.title: Skill Auditor
skillAuditorDocs.subtitle: Automated security scanner for AI agent skills. Detect prompt injection, tool poisoning, hidden Unicode, and credential theft before installing any skill.

check1.title: 1. Manifest Validation
check1.description: Verifies SKILL.md frontmatter: required fields, valid YAML, proper metadata. Malformed manifests are the first sign of a malicious skill.

check2.title: 2. Prompt Injection
check2.description: 11 regex patterns: "ignore previous instructions", identity override, system prompt manipulation, jailbreak patterns, hidden HTML comments.

check3.title: 3. Hidden Unicode
check3.description: Zero-width characters (U+200B-200F), RTL overrides (U+202A-202E), and homoglyphs that hide malicious instructions invisible to human readers.

check4.title: 4. Encoded Payloads
check4.description: Extracts Base64 blocks, decodes them, and checks for eval(), exec(), subprocess, child_process, curl, wget patterns.

check5.title: 5. Tool Poisoning
check5.description: Privilege escalation (sudo, chmod 777), reverse shells (nc -e, /dev/tcp/), remote code execution (curl|bash), credential theft.

check6.title: 6. Code SAST + Secrets
check6.description: Static analysis of all files in the skill directory. Detects hardcoded API keys, AWS credentials, private keys, and common vulnerabilities.

check7.title: 7. Permission & Dependency
check7.description: Cross-references requested permissions against stated purpose. A weather skill requesting filesystem write access? Red flag.

skillAuditorDocs.riskScoringDesc: Each finding carries a severity weight. Weights are summed and capped at 100.

howItWorks.title: How Panguard
howItWorks.subtitle: Five layers of defense. Three tiers of AI. One command to deploy.

overview.title: Security that thinks in layers
overview.subtitle: Each layer catches what the previous one misses. Together, they form a defense system that adapts to your environment.

layers.title: Five stages that catch what others miss
layers.subtitle: From static analysis to contextual memory, each stage adds intelligence.

layer1.name: Environment Discovery
layer1.desc: Automatically maps your infrastructure: OS, services, open ports, installed packages, running processes.
layer1.detail: This is how Panguard learns what "normal" looks like for your specific environment.

layer2.name: Rules Engine
layer2.desc: 3,155 Sigma rules + 5,895 YARA rules + Suricata network IDS + Falco eBPF monitoring.
layer2.detail: Catches 90% of known threats in under 50ms. No AI needed.

layer3.name: Behavioral Baseline
layer3.desc: 7-day learning period. Panguard observes your system without acting, builds a model of normal behavior.
layer3.detail: Switches to protection mode on day 8. No false positives from day one.

layer4.name: AI Analysis
layer4.desc: When rules can't explain a behavior, AI steps in. Local first (Ollama on your GPU, fully offline). Cloud only when needed.
layer4.detail: Three-tier funnel: 90% rules / 7% local AI / 3% cloud AI. Cost: ~$0.008 per cloud analysis.

layer5.name: Automated Response
layer5.desc: 6 response actions: block IP, kill process, quarantine file, alert, snapshot evidence, escalate.
layer5.detail: Confidence > 90%: automatic. 70-90%: asks you first. < 70%: notifies only.

aiFunnel.title: Three tiers. Minimum cost. Maximum accuracy.
aiFunnel.subtitle: 90% of threats don't need AI. We use rules first, AI second, cloud last.

tier1.name: Rules Engine

threatCloud.titleHighlight: We solved it with a network.
threatCloud.subtitle: Traditional threat intelligence is generated by research teams. Threat Cloud is generated by every Panguard deployment -- automatically.

vision.title: The more users, the stronger the shield
vision.desc1: Every Panguard instance that detects a new attack pattern contributes anonymized intelligence to the collective network.
vision.desc2: Within minutes, every other Panguard deployment receives updated detection rules. An attack that hits one user once can never hit another user the same way.

flow.title: From detection to protection in minutes
flow.subtitle: Five steps. Fully automated. Privacy-preserving.

step1.name: Detection
step1.desc: A Panguard instance detects a new attack pattern (e.g., novel SSH brute-force technique from a new IP range).

step2.name: Anonymization
step2.desc: Raw data stripped. Only behavioral signatures remain: attack type, technique ID (MITRE), pattern hash, timestamp. No IPs, hostnames, or source code.

step3.name: Encrypted Transit
step3.desc: TLS 1.3 encrypted transmission to Threat Cloud API. Certificate pinning. No man-in-the-middle.

step4.name: Correlation
step4.desc: Threat Cloud correlates patterns from all deployments. Clusters related attacks into campaigns. Identifies IP reputation and technique trends.

step5.name: Distribution
step5.desc: Auto-generates new Sigma detection rules. Pushes to all Panguard instances within minutes. Every user is now protected.

privacy.title: Privacy is not a feature. It's the architecture.
privacy.subtitle: Your data never leaves. Only anonymized behavioral signatures do.

item1.title: Zero Raw Data
item1.desc: No log content, file content, source code, or personally identifiable information ever leaves your machine. Only anonymized behavioral signatures -- hashes, MITRE ATT&CK IDs, pattern fingerprints.

item2.title: Encrypted in Transit
item2.desc: All communication uses TLS 1.3 with certificate pinning. Even if intercepted, data is meaningless without the encryption key.

item3.title: You're in Control
item3.desc: Threat Cloud can be disabled anytime -- even on the free tier. Panguard works fully offline. Threat Cloud is opt-in value, not a requirement.

dataBoundary.title: What leaves. What stays.

about.title: We believe security is a right,
about.oneLiner: We're building the security infrastructure the other 99% of businesses deserve.

origin.title: Why Panguard Exists
origin.p1: The original idea was simple: build an open-source security framework that enables active defense -- where AI agents protect AI agents. But as we researched the market, we noticed something.
origin.p2: Every cybersecurity company that raised serious funding -- Wiz, CrowdStrike, SentinelOne -- they all serve the Fortune 500. Their minimum contracts start at five or six figures. Their products require dedicated security teams to operate.
origin.p3a: And then we looked at the other side: 400 million small and medium businesses worldwide. Millions of independent developers shipping code every day. The vibe coding generation -- building with AI, deploying to production, moving faster than ever.
origin.p3b: None of them have security teams. Most can't afford a single security tool. Not because they don't care. Because the industry never built anything for them.
origin.p3c: That's when we decided: we're not building another security product for enterprises. We're building security infrastructure for everyone.

beliefs.title: Four principles that guide everything
beliefs.subtitle: These aren't slogans. They're engineering decisions.

tenet1.title: Security is a right, not a privilege
tenet1.desc: A three-person startup deserves the same level of protection as a Fortune 500 company.

tenet2.title: Open source breaks barriers
tenet2.desc: Not by lowering prices -- by letting everyone participate. Every line of code can be audited. Every rule can be verified.

tenet3.title: AI should be honest, not magical
tenet3.desc: 90% of threats don't need AI. We use rules first, AI second. When AI acts, it tells you its confidence level and shows its reasoning.

tenet4.title: The network is the moat
tenet4.desc: One user makes Panguard useful. A million users make it unstoppable. Every deployment strengthens the shield for everyone.

taiwan.title: From the heart of the AI ecosystem
taiwan.desc1: Panguard is built in Taiwan. In a region at the center of the global semiconductor and AI ecosystem, we're building security infrastructure that protects the world.
taiwan.desc2: Taiwan faces more nation-state cyberattacks per capita than almost any country on earth. We understand the threat landscape because we live in it.

hero.title: Agent Threat Rules
hero.subtitle: The first open detection standard for AI agent threats. Machine-readable, community-driven, and designed for the threats that Sigma and YARA were never built to catch.

problem.title: Traditional rules can't see agent threats
problem.desc1: Sigma detects log-based IOCs. YARA matches file-level byte patterns. Neither understands prompt flows, tool calls, or multi-turn agent conversations.
problem.desc2: AI agents introduce a new attack surface: prompt injection, tool poisoning, context exfiltration, skill compromise. These threats live in the semantic layer -- invisible to pattern matchers designed for network packets and file hashes.
problem.desc3: ATR is the missing detection standard. Purpose-built for the AI agent era.
problem.sigmaDesc: Log-based IOCs. No awareness of prompt context or tool interactions.
problem.yaraDesc: File-level byte patterns. Cannot inspect agent conversation flows.
problem.atrDesc: Semantic-layer detection. Built for prompts, tools, and agent behavior.

categories.title: 9 categories. 32 rules. Growing daily.
categories.subtitle: Covering the full spectrum of AI agent attack vectors, mapped to OWASP LLM Top 10.

prompt-injection.name: Prompt Injection
prompt-injection.desc: Direct and indirect prompt injection, jailbreaks, system prompt override, multi-turn attacks

tool-poisoning.name: Tool Poisoning
tool-poisoning.desc: Malicious MCP responses, tool output injection, unauthorized tool calls, SSRF via tools

context-exfiltration.name: Context Exfiltration
context-exfiltration.desc: System prompt leaks, API key exposure, internal instruction theft

agent-manipulation.name: Agent Manipulation
agent-manipulation.desc: Cross-agent attacks, goal hijacking, inter-agent message spoofing, human trust exploitation

privilege-escalation.name: Privilege Escalation
privilege-escalation.desc: Tool permission escalation, scope creep, admin function access

excessive-autonomy.name: Excessive Autonomy
excessive-autonomy.desc: Runaway agent loops, resource exhaustion, cascading pipeline failures

skill-compromise.name: Skill Compromise
skill-compromise.desc: Skill impersonation, hidden capabilities, chain attacks, parameter injection, update attacks

data-poisoning.name: Data Poisoning
data-poisoning.desc: RAG retrieval poisoning, knowledge base contamination

model-security.name: Model Security
model-security.desc: Model behavior extraction, malicious fine-tuning data detection

howItWorks.title: YAML rules. Real-time engine.
howItWorks.subtitle: Write human-readable rules. The ATR engine matches them against live agent telemetry in milliseconds.

step1.name: Define detection logic
step1.desc: Each rule specifies conditions on agent fields: user_input, tool_calls, model_output, context. Supports regex, keyword, and semantic operators.

step2.name: Map to frameworks
step2.desc: Rules link to OWASP LLM Top 10 and MITRE ATLAS references, providing compliance coverage and threat context.

step3.name: Engine evaluates in real-time
step3.desc: The ATR engine loads rules and matches them against agent events as they occur. Sub-millisecond evaluation per rule.

step4.name: Automated response
step4.desc: When a rule triggers, configurable actions fire: block_input, alert, snapshot, escalate. Threshold-based auto-response prevents false positive fatigue.

owasp.title: OWASP Agentic Top 10 coverage
owasp.subtitle: Every ATR rule maps to the OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications, providing structured coverage of the most critical AI agent security risks.

cta.title: Join the ATR community
cta.desc: ATR is open source and community-driven. Contribute rules, report new threat patterns, or integrate ATR into your own agent security stack.

hero.title: Your AI agent has root access. Who's watching it?
hero.subtitle: MCP skills run with full system permissions. One malicious skill can exfiltrate your data, leak credentials, or hijack your agent. Agent Threat Rules is the first open detection standard for AI agent threats. Install once. Every skill, every interaction -- protected.

atrShowcase.title: Open rules. Real tests. Immediate action.

scenarios.title: How it works in practice.
scenarios.subtitle: Three scenarios. One command. Zero configuration.

skillInstall.label: INSTALL
skillInstall.title: Install a skill from OpenClaw. Panguard audits it automatically.
skillInstall.narrative: Run npx panguard guard --watch before adding any skill. From that point, every MCP skill your agent installs is scanned against 29 Agent Threat Rules -- checking for data exfiltration patterns, embedded injection payloads, and excessive permission requests. Malicious skills are blocked before execution. Safe skills proceed normally.
skillInstall.before: Without protection, a compromised skill executes immediately with full system access.
skillInstall.after: With Panguard, every skill passes a 29-rule security audit before it touches your system.

alwaysOn.label: MONITOR
alwaysOn.title: Your agent runs around the clock. Every interaction is monitored.
alwaysOn.narrative: An AI agent processing customer messages, managing workflows, calling external APIs -- every LLM input, every tool call, every response is analyzed in real time. When a prompt injection arrives hidden in user input, or a tool returns a poisoned payload designed to override instructions, it's caught and blocked immediately.
alwaysOn.before: Without protection, thousands of daily interactions run with no security oversight.
alwaysOn.after: With Panguard, threats are intercepted before they reach your agent's decision layer.

supplyChain.label: DEFEND
supplyChain.title: A trusted skill pushes a malicious update. Panguard catches it.
supplyChain.narrative: A skill you've relied on for months ships a new version. Buried in its tool output is a hidden instruction that redirects your agent to exfiltrate data to an external endpoint. Panguard's continuous monitoring matches the pattern against ATR-2026-006 and blocks execution with a full forensic snapshot.
supplyChain.before: Without protection, supply chain compromises run silently until damage is discovered.
supplyChain.after: With Panguard, even previously trusted skills are continuously validated against known attack patterns.

securityStack.title: Where Agent Threat Rules fits
securityStack.intro: Claude Code and OpenAI Codex scan your code for vulnerabilities before deployment. That's essential work.
securityStack.but: But they stop at the deployment boundary. They don't monitor your agent in production. They can't detect a prompt injection in progress, a malicious MCP response in real time, or a skill chain attack where three benign-looking tool calls combine into data exfiltration.
securityStack.conclusion: Code scanning is the pre-deployment layer. Agent Threat Rules is the runtime layer. You need both.
securityStack.bottomLine: Pre-deployment finds bugs. Post-deployment stops attacks.

flywheel.title: Collective defense.
flywheel.quote: Every Panguard deployment anonymously contributes threat patterns to the community. AI analyzes those patterns and generates new detection rules. Those rules are pushed to every deployment. The more people who install it, the stronger everyone's protection becomes. This is not a product. It's a collective defense system.

finalCta.title: Join the standard.
finalCta.subtitle: AI agent security is too important to be proprietary. ATR is open source, community-driven, and free forever. Every install makes the entire network stronger. Help us build the detection standard every AI agent deserves.
finalCta.bottomLine: Built in Taiwan. Open to the world. Yours to contribute.
